ingredients: ½ stick butter (optional and not pictured)

steps: Preheat oven to 400 degrees. In a small bowl, combine cornmeal and milk. Let it rest for at least 5 minutes.

Grease a 12-inch cast iron pan. (If you do not have a 12-inch pan, this recipe works well with a 13 x 9 inch baking pan. A 12-inch pan is about 113 square inches, while a 13 x 9 inch pan is 117 square inches. Pretty darn close.)
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, salt, and sugar. Mix in the cornmeal mixture, eggs, and oil until smooth.

This is an optional step that I just started using recently. It isn't necessary, but it does produce a much richer tasting cornbread. While you are whisking the ingredients above, put ½ stick of butter in the prepared cast-iron skillet and put it in the oven until the butter is melted and bubbling. Remove the skillet from the oven and go to the next step. Note: Because the pan will be hot when you add the batter, test the cornbread five minutes earlier than is directed at the end of this post.
Pour batter into the prepared pan.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a knife inserted into the center of the cornbread comes out clean.
